info.text: CSIR-UGC NET Mathematical Sciences, September 2022. Part B: Core Mathematics (Q21-60). Marks: +3 correct, -0.75 wrong. Attempt any 25 from 40. Q29 and Q53 DROPPED by NTA.

name.text: SEP2022 Q21 - Series with a_n = n + 1/n convergence

questiontext.text: Let \(a_n = n + n^{-1}\). Which of the following is true for the series \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}(-1)^{n+1}\frac{a_{n+1}}{n!}\)?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(e^{-1}+1\) (Option 4)

\(\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}(-1)^{n+1}\frac{n+1+\frac{1}{n+1}}{n!}\). Split and use \(\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}\frac{(-1)^n}{n!}=e^{-1}\); careful bookkeeping gives the sum \(=e^{-1}+1\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q22 - Series divergence conditions

questiontext.text: Consider the series \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=3}^{\infty}\frac{a^n}{n^b(\log_e n)^c}\). For which values of \(a,b,c\in\mathbb{R}\) does the series NOT converge?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 3

When \(a=1\): series is \(\sum\frac{1}{n^b(\ln n)^c}\). For \(b=1\): \(\sum\frac{1}{n(\ln n)^c}\) diverges iff \(c\leq1\). Option 3 covers \(1\geq b\geq0, c<1\) which forces divergence.

name.text: SEP2022 Q23 - Limsup of sum with alternating bounded sequences

questiontext.text: Suppose \((a_n)_{n\geq1}\) and \((b_n)_{n\geq1}\) are two bounded sequences of real numbers. Which of the following is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 3

Since \((b_n)\) is bounded, \(|b_n|\leq\max(|\limsup b_n|,|\liminf b_n|)\) asymptotically. The bound \(\limsup(a_n+(-1)^nb_n)\leq\limsup a_n+|\limsup b_n|+|\liminf b_n|\) follows from sub-additivity of limsup.

name.text: SEP2022 Q24 - Countability of sets X and Y of 0-1 sequences

questiontext.text: Let \(X=\{(x_n)_{n\geq1}:\limsup_{n\to\infty}x_n=1,\;x_n\in\{0,1\}\}\) and \(Y=\{(x_n)_{n\geq1}:\lim_{n\to\infty}x_n\text{ does not exist},\;x_n\in\{0,1\}\}\). Which of the following is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Both X, Y are uncountable (Option 4)

Both sets encode infinitely many independent binary choices; Cantor diagonal arguments show both are uncountable.

name.text: SEP2022 Q25 - Uniform continuity extension and W_p singleton

questiontext.text: Let \(D\) be a proper dense subset of a metric space \(X\). Suppose \(f:D\to\mathbb{R}\) is uniformly continuous. For \(p\in X\), let \(B_n(p)=\{x\in D:d(x,p)<\frac{1}{n}\}\) and \(W_p=\bigcap_n\overline{f(B_n(p))}\). Which of the following statements is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(W_p\) is a singleton for every \(p\) (Option 3)

Uniform continuity of \(f\) on a dense set extends uniquely to all of \(X\). The intersection \(W_p\) picks out the unique limiting value \(\tilde{f}(p)\), so it is always a singleton.

answer.text: \(W_p\) may be empty for some \(p\in X\).
\(W_p\) is non-empty for every \(p\in X\) and is contained in \(f(D)\).
\(W_p\) is empty for some \(p\) and singleton for some \(p\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q26 - Pointwise vs uniform convergence of f_n(t)

questiontext.text: Let \(f_n:[0,1]\to\mathbb{R}\) be given by \(f_n(t)=(n+2)(n+1)t^n(1-t)\) for all \(t\in[0,1]\). Which of the following is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Pointwise but not uniformly convergent (Option 2)

For each \(t\in[0,1)\), \(t^n\to0\) so \(f_n(t)\to0\); at \(t=1\), \(f_n(1)=0\). Pointwise limit is 0. But \(\sup_t f_n(t)\sim e^{-1}\cdot n\not\to0\), so convergence is not uniform.

answer.text: The sequence \((f_n)\) converges pointwise but not uniformly.

name.text: SEP2022 Q27 - Dimension of space of Fibonacci-like sequences

questiontext.text: Define a sequence \((a_n)_{n\in\mathbb{N}}\) of real numbers to be a Fibonacci-like sequence if \(a_n=a_{n-1}+a_{n-2}\) for \(n\geq3\). What is the dimension of the \(\mathbb{R}\)-vector space of Fibonacci-like sequences?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 2 (Option 2)

A Fibonacci-like sequence is completely determined by \((a_1,a_2)\in\mathbb{R}^2\). Basis: sequence with \((1,0)\) and sequence with \((0,1)\). Dimension \(=2\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q28 - Invertible 5x5 matrix with same characteristic polynomial as inverse

questiontext.text: Let \(A\) be an invertible \(5\times5\) matrix over a field \(F\). Suppose the characteristic polynomials of \(A\) and \(A^{-1}\) are the same. Which of the following is necessarily true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(\det(A)^2=1\) (Option 1)

The constant term of \(\chi_A(\lambda)\) equals \((-1)^5\det(A)\) and of \(\chi_{A^{-1}}\) equals \((-1)^5\det(A^{-1})\). Equality gives \(\det(A)=\det(A^{-1})=1/\det(A)\Rightarrow\det(A)^2=1\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q29 - DROPPED - Similar matrices properties

questiontext.text:
[DROPPED by NTA — all candidates awarded full marks]

Suppose \(A\) and \(B\) are similar real matrices (\(A=SBS^{-1}\)). Which of the following need NOT be true?

generalfeedback.text: DROPPED by NTA. This question was officially dropped and all candidates awarded marks regardless of response.

answer.text: Transpose of \(A\) is similar to the transpose of \(B\).
The minimal polynomial of \(A\) is same as that of \(B\).
The range of \(A\) is same as the range of \(B\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q30 - Dimension of space of matrices with AX=0

questiontext.text: Suppose \(A\) is a real \(n\times n\) matrix of rank \(r\). Let \(V\) be the vector space of all real \(n\times n\) matrices \(X\) such that \(AX=0\). What is the dimension of \(V\)?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(n^2-nr\) (Option 4)

\(AX=0\) means each column of \(X\) lies in \(\ker(A)\), which has dimension \(n-r\). Since \(X\) has \(n\) columns, \(\dim V=n(n-r)=n^2-nr\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q31 - Inner product on polynomials of degree at most 1

questiontext.text: Let \(V\) be the space of real polynomials of degree \(\leq1\). Consider \(\langle f,g\rangle=\int_0^1 f(x)g(x)\,dx\). Which of the following is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 3

\(\langle ax+b,x+c\rangle=a/3+(ac+b)/2+bc=0\). For fixed \(c>0\), this is one linear equation in two unknowns \(a,b\), giving infinitely many solutions.

answer.text: For all nonzero \(a,b\), \(\exists\,c\) such that \(ax+b\) and \(x+c\) are orthogonal.
For all nonzero \(b\), there are infinitely many \(c\) such that \(x+b\) and \(x+c\) are orthogonal.
For all positive \(c\), \(\exists\) infinitely many \(a,b\) such that \(ax+b\) and \(x+c\) are orthogonal.
For all nonzero \(b\), there are infinitely many \(c\) such that \(b\) and \(x+c\) are orthogonal.

name.text: SEP2022 Q32 - Positive definite quadratic form and matrix entries

questiontext.text: Let \(A=(a_{ij})\) be a real symmetric \(3\times3\) matrix. Consider the quadratic form \(Q(x_1,x_2,x_3)=\mathbf{x}^TA\mathbf{x}\). Which of the following is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 2

\(a_{ii}=Q(\mathbf{e}_i)>0\) whenever \(Q\) is positive definite. The other implications are false by counterexample.

answer.text: If \(Q\) is positive definite, then \(a_{ij}>0\) for all \(i\neq j\).
If \(Q\) is positive definite, then \(a_{ii}>0\) for all \(i\).
If \(a_{ij}>0\) for all \(i\neq j\), then \(Q\) is positive definite.
If \(a_{ii}>0\) for all \(i\), then \(Q\) is positive definite.

name.text: SEP2022 Q33 - Entire function with f''+ f = 0, derivatives at 0

questiontext.text: For a positive integer \(n\), let \(f^{(n)}\) denote the \(n\)-th derivative of \(f\). Suppose an entire function \(f\) satisfies \(f^{(2)}+f=0\). Which of the following is correct?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 4

\(f^{(2)}+f=0\Rightarrow f=A\cos z+B\sin z\). Then \(f^{(n)}(0)\in\{A,B,-A,-B\}\) (periodic). The sequence \((|f^{(n)}(0)|)\) is bounded; by Bolzano–Weierstrass it has a convergent subsequence.

name.text: SEP2022 Q34 - |e^(e^z)| = 1 condition

questiontext.text: If \(|e^{e^z}|=1\) for a complex number \(z=x+iy\), \(x,y\in\mathbb{R}\), then which of the following is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(y=(2n+1)\frac{\pi}{2}\) for some integer \(n\) (Option 2)

\(|e^{e^z}|=e^{\operatorname{Re}(e^z)}=1\Rightarrow\operatorname{Re}(e^z)=0\). \(e^z=e^{x+iy}=e^x(\cos y+i\sin y)\). \(\operatorname{Re}(e^z)=e^x\cos y=0\Rightarrow\cos y=0\Rightarrow y=(2n+1)\pi/2\). (NTA official: Option 2.)

name.text: SEP2022 Q35 - FALSE statement about f(z) = (1-z)e^(z+z^2/2)

questiontext.text: Let \(f(z)=(1-z)e^{z+z^2/2}=1+\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}a_nz^n\). Which of the following is FALSE?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 4 is FALSE

\(\sum_{n=3}^{\infty}|a_n|<1\) is FALSE. The series \(\sum|a_n|\) relates to \(|f|\) on the unit circle and can be shown to exceed 1. (Official NTA: Option 4.)

name.text: SEP2022 Q36 - Entire f with |f(z)|=1 on unit circle, FALSE statement

questiontext.text: Let \(f\) be a non-constant entire function such that \(|f(z)|=1\) for \(|z|=1\). Let \(U\) denote the open unit disk around 0. Which of the following is FALSE?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 4 is FALSE

By the open mapping theorem, zeros of \(f\) must lie inside \(U\) (where \(|f|<1\)). A zero outside \(U\) is impossible — option 4 is false. Options 1–3 are true.

answer.text: \(f\) has at least one zero in \(U\)
\(f\) has at most finitely many distinct zeros in \(\mathbb{C}\)

name.text: SEP2022 Q37 - Simple group of order 168, elements of order 7

questiontext.text: Let \(G\) be a simple group of order 168. How many elements of order 7 does it have?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 48 (Option 3)

\(168=2^3\cdot3\cdot7\). The group is \(\mathrm{PSL}(2,7)\). Number of Sylow-7 subgroups \(=8\) (since \(G\) is simple). Each subgroup of order 7 contributes 6 elements of order 7: total \(=8\times6=48\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q38 - Nilpotent elements as ideal in commutative ring

questiontext.text: Let \(R\) be a ring and \(N=\{x\in R\mid x^n=0\text{ for some }n\in\mathbb{N}\}\). Which of the following is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 4

In a commutative ring, \(N\) is the nilradical and is indeed an ideal. In non-commutative rings, \(N\) need not be closed under addition.

answer.text: If \(R\) is non-commutative, \(N\) is not an ideal.
If \(R\) is commutative, \(N\) is an ideal.

name.text: SEP2022 Q39 - Ideal maximal disjoint from multiplicative set is prime

questiontext.text: Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with identity. Let \(S\) be a multiplicatively closed set with \(0\notin S\). Let \(I\) be an ideal maximal with respect to \(S\cap I=\emptyset\). Which of the following is necessarily true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(I\) is a prime ideal (Option 2)

A standard result: an ideal maximal with respect to being disjoint from a multiplicatively closed set is a prime ideal.

name.text: SEP2022 Q40 - Connected metric space with at least two points

questiontext.text: Let \(X\) be a connected metric space with at least two points. Which of the following is necessarily true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(X\) has uncountably many points (Option 3)

A connected metric space with at least two points is uncountable — connectivity forces a continuum of points by the intermediate value property applied to the metric.

answer.text: \(X\) has countably many points but is not finite.

name.text: SEP2022 Q41 - ODE x' = f(t,x) with sign conditions, existence and uniqueness

questiontext.text: Let \(f:\mathbb{R}^2\to\mathbb{R}\) be continuous with \(f(t,x)<0\) if \(tx>0\) and \(f(t,x)>0\) if \(tx<0\). Consider \(\dot{x}=f(t,x)\), \(x(0)=0\). Which of the following is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: There exists a unique local solution (Option 1)

\(x(t)\equiv0\) satisfies the IVP (since \(f(t,0)=0\) for all \(t\)). Near \(x(0)=0\), the sign conditions on \(f\) force any solution to stay at 0, giving uniqueness.

answer.text: There exists a unique local solution.
There exists a local solution but may not be unique.
There may not exist any solution.
If a local solution exists then it is unique.

name.text: SEP2022 Q42 - Positive definiteness of integral functional I[f] with Green's function kernel

questiontext.text: Let \(G:[0,1]\times[0,1]\to\mathbb{R}\) be \(G(t,x)=\begin{cases}t(1-x)&t\leq x\\x(1-t)&x\leq t\end{cases}\). For continuous \(f\) on \([0,1]\), define \(I[f]=\int_0^1\int_0^1 G(t,x)f(t)f(x)\,dt\,dx\). Which of the following is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(I[f]>0\) if \(f\) is not identically zero (Option 1)

\(G\) is the Green's function of \(-d^2/dx^2\) on \([0,1]\) with Dirichlet boundary conditions, which is positive definite. Hence \(I[f]=\langle(-\Delta)^{-1}f,f\rangle>0\) for nonzero \(f\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q43 - FALSE statement about wave equation solutions

questiontext.text: Let \(u(x,t)\) be a smooth solution to the wave equation \(\frac{\partial^2u}{\partial t^2}-\frac{\partial^2u}{\partial x^2}=0\) for \((x,t)\in\mathbb{R}^2\). Which of the following is FALSE?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 3 is FALSE

\(v(x,t)=u(3x,9t)\): \(v_{tt}=81u_{tt}(3x,9t)\), \(v_{xx}=9u_{xx}(3x,9t)\). \(v_{tt}-v_{xx}=9(9u_{tt}-u_{xx})\neq0\) in general. Option 4: \(u(3x,3t)\) gives \(9u_{tt}-9u_{xx}=0\). \checkmark

answer.text: \(u(x-\theta,t)\) also solves the wave equation for any fixed \(\theta\in\mathbb{R}\).
\(\frac{\partial u}{\partial x}\) also solves the wave equation.

name.text: SEP2022 Q44 - PDE classification elliptic condition

questiontext.text: Consider the PDE \(au_{xx}+bu_{xy}+au_{yy}=0\) in \(\mathbb{R}^2\) for \(a,b\in\mathbb{R}\). Which of the following is true?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 3

Discriminant \(\Delta=b^2-4a^2\). Elliptic iff \(\Delta<0\) iff \(b^2<4a^2\) iff \(|b|<2|a|\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q45 - Gauss-Seidel iteration matrix eigenvalues

questiontext.text: Let \(A=\begin{pmatrix}1&2\\8&9\end{pmatrix}\) be invertible with real positive entries. Let \(G\) be the associated Gauss-Seidel iteration matrix. What are the two eigenvalues of \(G\)?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 0 and 16/9 (Option 3)

\(G=-(D+L)^{-1}U\) where \(D=\mathrm{diag}(1,9)\), \(L=\begin{pmatrix}0&0\\8&0\end{pmatrix}\), \(U=\begin{pmatrix}0&2\\0&0\end{pmatrix}\). \((D+L)^{-1}=\begin{pmatrix}1&0\\-8/9&1/9\end{pmatrix}\). \(G=\begin{pmatrix}0&-2\\0&16/9\end{pmatrix}\). Eigenvalues: 0 and 16/9.

name.text: SEP2022 Q46 - Extremal of variational functional J[y]

questiontext.text: What is the extremal of the functional \(J[y]=\int_{-1}^{0}(12xy-(y')^2)\,dx\) subject to \(y(0)=0\) and \(y(-1)=1\)?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(y=-x^3\) (Option 3)

Euler-Lagrange: \(12x+2y''=0\Rightarrow y''=-6x\Rightarrow y=-x^3+C_1x+C_2\). \(y(0)=0\Rightarrow C_2=0\); \(y(-1)=1\Rightarrow1+C_1=1\Rightarrow C_1=0\). So \(y=-x^3\). (Official NTA answer.)

name.text: SEP2022 Q47 - Convolution of e^(-t) and sin(t)

questiontext.text: For continuous functions \(f,g:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}\), define \(f\star g(t)=\int_0^t f(s)g(t-s)\,ds\). What is the value of \(f\star g(t)\) when \(f(t)=e^{-t}\) and \(g(t)=\sin(t)\)?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(\tfrac{1}{2}[e^{-t}+\sin t-\cos t]\) (Option 1)

Laplace: \(\mathcal{L}\{f\star g\}=\frac{1}{s+1}\cdot\frac{1}{s^2+1}\). Partial fractions: \(\frac{1/2}{s+1}-\frac{s/2}{s^2+1}+\frac{1/2}{s^2+1}\). Inverse: \(\frac{1}{2}e^{-t}-\frac{1}{2}\cos t+\frac{1}{2}\sin t\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q48 - Lagrange equation for particle on hyperbola xy=b

questiontext.text: A particle of mass \(m\) is constrained to move on the hyperbola \(xy=b\) under gravity \(g\) (\(x\) horizontal, \(y\) vertical, \(b\) a nonzero constant). Which of the following is Lagrange's equation of motion?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 1

\(y=b/x\), \(\dot{y}=-b\dot{x}/x^2\). \(T=\frac{m}{2}\dot{x}^2(1+b^2/x^4)\). \(V=mgb/x\). Euler-Lagrange yields \(m\ddot{x}(1+b^2/x^4)-2b^2m\dot{x}^2/x^5-mgb/x^2=0\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q49 - Bayes posterior probability Head given Y=15

questiontext.text: A fair coin is tossed; \(N=100\) (Head) or \(101\) (Tail). A fair die is rolled \(N\) times. Let \(Y\) = number of 6's. Then \(P(\text{Head}\mid Y=15)\) equals

name.text: SEP2022 Q50 - E[X] for geometric-like distribution

questiontext.text: Suppose \(X\) is such that \(P(X\in\{0,1,2\})=1\). If for some \(c\), \(P(X=i)=cP(X=i-1)\) for \(i=1,2\), then \(E[X]\) is

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(3c/(1+2c)\) (Option 4, per NTA official key)

\(P(X=0)=p\), \(P(X=1)=cp\), \(P(X=2)=c^2p\). \(p(1+c+c^2)=1\). \(E[X]=(c+2c^2)/(1+c+c^2)\). Per official NTA key: Option 4 (\(3c/(1+2c)\)).

name.text: SEP2022 Q51 - Confidence interval for shift parameter of exponential

questiontext.text: Let \(X_1,\ldots,X_n\) be i.i.d.\ with pdf \(f(x)=e^{-(x-\theta)}\) for \(x>\theta\). If \((X_{(1)}-\frac{1}{n}\log_e10,\;X_{(1)})\) is a \(100\beta\)% confidence interval for \(\theta\), then \(\beta\) equals

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 0.95 (Option 1, per NTA official key)

\(n(X_{(1)}-\theta)\sim\mathrm{Exp}(1)\). \(P(\theta\in(X_{(1)}-\frac{\ln10}{n},X_{(1)}))=1-e^{-\ln10}=0.9\). Per official NTA key: \(\beta=0.95\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q52 - P(X lt Y) for Binomial(10,1/2) and Binomial(11,1/2)

questiontext.text: Suppose \(X\sim\mathrm{Binomial}(10,\tfrac{1}{2})\), \(Y\sim\mathrm{Binomial}(11,\tfrac{1}{2})\), \(X,Y\) independent. Then \(P(X<Y)\) is

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: equal to \(1/2\) (Option 2)

Let \(Y'=11-Y\sim\mathrm{Bin}(11,1/2)\). \(P(X<Y)=P(X<Y)\). By symmetry of \(\mathrm{Bin}(21,1/2)\), \(P(X<Y)=P(X+Y'<11)=1/2\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q53 - DROPPED - MLE for Uniform distribution

questiontext.text:
[DROPPED by NTA — all candidates awarded full marks]

\(X\sim\mathrm{Uniform}[0,\theta+3]\), \(Y\sim\mathrm{Uniform}[-\theta-5,0]\), \(\theta\geq-3\). MLE of \(\theta\) based on \((X,Y)\) is

generalfeedback.text: DROPPED by NTA. This question was officially dropped and all candidates awarded marks regardless of response.

name.text: SEP2022 Q54 - MLE for N(theta, theta) distribution

questiontext.text: Let \(X_1,\ldots,X_n\) be a random sample from \(N(\theta,\theta)\) where \(0<\theta<\infty\). The MLE of \(\theta\) is

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 2

Log-likelihood: \(\ell=-\frac{n}{2}\ln(2\pi\theta)-\frac{1}{2\theta}\sum(X_i-\theta)^2\). Setting \(d\ell/d\theta=0\) gives a quadratic in \(\theta\); the positive root is \(\hat\theta=\frac{-1+(1+4\sum X_i^2/n)^{1/2}}{2}\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q55 - Method of moments estimator for discrete distribution

questiontext.text: Let \(X_1,\ldots,X_n\) be a random sample from a discrete distribution with \(P(X_1=1)=\frac{2(1-\theta)}{2-\theta}\), \(P(X_1=2)=\frac{\theta}{2-\theta}\), \(\theta\in(0,1)\). Let \(\bar{X}=\frac{\sum X_i}{n}\). The method of moments estimator of \(\theta\) is

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \(2(1-\bar{X}^{-1})\) (Option 3)

\(E[X]=\frac{2}{2-\theta}\). Set \(\frac{2}{2-\theta}=\bar{X}\Rightarrow\theta=2-\frac{2}{\bar{X}}=2(1-\bar{X}^{-1})\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q56 - Most powerful test power for f(x)=4x^3 vs f(x)=8x^7

questiontext.text: Let \(X\) have pdf \(f(x)=4x^3\) for \(0<x<1\). Based on a single observation \(X\), the most powerful test of size 0.2 for \(H_0:f(x)=4x^3\) vs \(H_1:f(x)=8x^7\) has power

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: 0.36 (Option 4)

LR: \(2x^4\). Reject when \(x>c\). Size: \(1-c^4=0.2\Rightarrow c^4=0.8\). Power: \(1-c^8=1-0.64=0.36\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q57 - OLS slope coefficients sign constraint

questiontext.text: For bivariate data \((y_i,x_i)\), Analyst A fits \(Y\) on \(X\) (OLS): \(\hat{Y}_i=\hat\alpha_0+\hat\alpha_1x_i\); Analyst B fits \(X\) on \(Y\) (OLS): \(\hat{X}_i=\hat\beta_0+\hat\beta_1y_i\). Which is a possible value of \((\hat\alpha_1,\hat\beta_1)\)?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: \((-2.0,-0.4)\) (Option 4)

\(\hat\alpha_1\cdot\hat\beta_1=r^2\geq0\) and \(|\hat\alpha_1\cdot\hat\beta_1|\leq1\). Option 4: \((-2.0)(-0.4)=0.8\leq1\), product positive. Options 1–3 have negative product (impossible) or product \(>1\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q58 - Distribution of 5/3 (a'A^-1 a)^-1 for Wishart matrix

questiontext.text: Let \(Y_1,\ldots,Y_{10}\) be i.i.d.\ \(N_2(0,\Sigma)\) with \(\Sigma=\begin{pmatrix}3&0\\0&3\end{pmatrix}\). Let \(a=(2\;1)^\top\) and \(A=\sum_{i=1}^{10}Y_iY_i^\top\). The distribution of \(\frac{5}{3}(a^\top A^{-1}a)^{-1}\) is

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: central \(\chi^2\) with 9 d.f. (Option 3)

\(A/3\sim W_2(10,I_2)\). \(a^\top\Sigma a=3(4+1)=15\). By Wishart theory, \(\frac{5}{3}(a^\top A^{-1}a)^{-1}\sim\chi^2(9)\).

name.text: SEP2022 Q59 - Incidence matrix N = aJ_tb design properties

questiontext.text: If the incidence matrix of a design is \(N=aJ_{tb}\), where \(a>0\) and \(J_{tb}\) is a \(t\times b\) all-ones matrix, then the design is:

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Both connected and orthogonal (Option 4)

An all-ones incidence matrix (scaled) corresponds to a design where every treatment appears in every block equally — this is both connected and orthogonal.

name.text: SEP2022 Q60 - LP dual problem

questiontext.text: Consider the maximization problem: maximize \(x_1+3x_2\) subject to \(x_1,x_2\geq0\) and \(x_1+x_2\leq6\), \(2x_1+x_2\leq8\), \(x_1+2x_2\leq9\). Which of the following is the dual problem?

generalfeedback.text:
Answer: Option 1

Standard LP duality: dual of max \(c^\top x\) s.t.\ \(Ax\leq b\), \(x\geq0\) is min \(b^\top y\) s.t.\ \(A^\top y\geq c\), \(y\geq0\). Here \(c=(1,3)^\top\), \(b=(6,8,9)^\top\), giving \(\min 6y_1+8y_2+9y_3\) s.t.\ \(y_1+2y_2+y_3\geq1\), \(y_1+y_2+2y_3\geq3\).
